title: How is the Entry Plug inserted
display_title: How is the Entry Plug inserted
wikitext:
[]: How is the Entry-Plug inserted?             Quote Post by Zephros » Sun May 09, 2010 8:11 pm Well, I was wondering something the other day about Eva. The conclusion I came to was rather contradictory to what I had assumed Eva actually was. As far as I recall from canon Eva was essentially a giant human with an AT Field, I think Ritsuko said something to that effect. Even if that was not true, them being angel/human hybrids or whatever, I'm still confused. Here's why. How can a completely organic being have a metal rod shoved up their ass(Okay, not ass, but into their spine)? The only conclusion I could come up with is that there was some bio-circuitry inside the Evangelion, part of the armor perchance, that acts as conduit between the entry-plug and Evangelion. So they can't be entirely organic. They must have cybernetic implants. Thoughts? Has some conclusion in canon been told and I missed it?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 09, 2010 8:27 pm I've always thought the nature of the Evas as being cybernetic was pretty clear and concrete. They're not robots, and they're not giant humans inside of a metallic shell. As for the entry plug, it enters the body up near the top of the spinal column, and feeds down diagonally to the core which is located sort of at the base of the rib cage. I'd show you a diagram, but I don't have an image handy.

[]:             Quote Post by Zephros » Sun May 09, 2010 9:01 pm I found a thread related to it and saw the picture. I always assumed they were just humans, hehe. Also, could you or another mod or admin move this to Evangelion Discussion? I goofed at where I posted it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Sun May 09, 2010 10:48 pm RE: the thread title As carefully as possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon May 10, 2010 2:07 pm re title: With a candlelight dinner, a Barry Manilow CD, and lots of foreplay.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Mon May 10, 2010 9:35 pm Lucretius wrote: re title: With a candlelight dinner, a Barry Manilow CD, and lots of foreplay.

[]:             Quote Post by Frozzy » Tue May 11, 2010 8:19 am but if the entry plug is situated in the chest then what exactly is the veiw point of the pilot in terms of how you see how to control said eva if they are different levels to the head ?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue May 11, 2010 8:51 am Frozzy wrote: but if the entry plug is situated in the chest then what exactly is the veiw point of the pilot in terms of how you see how to control said eva if they are different levels to the head ?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 11, 2010 2:43 pm Chest seems to be more often then not the preferred cockpit location in humongous mecha, if only because there's more room there. Head and backpack are the only other commonly used pilot locations that come to mind unless there's a gestalt form involved.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Tue May 18, 2010 2:02 pm Sometimes gently and tenderly. Sometimes rough and hard.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue May 18, 2010 2:07 pm SenorSquiid wrote: Sometimes gently and tenderly. Sometimes rough and hard.
